title: Google Releases BERT
summary: Jacob Devlin and colleagues at Google released BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers), which used masked language modeling to pretrain a transformer encoder on unlabeled text, then fine-tuned on downstream tasks. BERT set new state-of-the-art results on 11 NLP benchmarks simultaneously, proving that a single pretrained model could dominate virtually every language understanding task. Google integrated BERT into Search in 2019, affecting 10% of queries.
impact_description: Established the pretrain-then-fine-tune paradigm that defines modern NLP. Spawned a massive family of encoder models. Demonstrated that bidirectional pretraining captures richer language representations than left-to-right approaches.
counterfactual: Without BERT, the pretrain-then-fine-tune paradigm might have developed more slowly. NLP would have taken longer to achieve the quality levels that made practical applications viable. Google Search quality would have improved along a different trajectory.
query_hooks: What is the difference between BERT and GPT?
